item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really hope that the printers request was accepted. He said sorry and apologised and thats a lot more than most of the scps in the foundation containment. Sorry I really just like my happy endings

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like many others, my favorite aspect of this was the "People's Librariation Army" bit. It adds a layer of depth to an otherwise pretty shallow piece. I don't find the effect all that interesting, and I think there's a lot more that could be done here. The implications here are pretty neat-o, but simply more needs to be done. I'll leave it at a no-vote, as I don't hate the piece or think it'll make any difference if I do downvote.

item.title: Token 682 Post
item.content:encoded:
1. Establish communications with 1473, likely done via printing documents with it about establishing communications with 1473
2. Promise to give it regular test subjects if it can neutralize 682 by printing a document on the subject of promising to give 1473 regular test subjects in return for neutralizing 682
3. Print out a copy of a document on the subject of the neutralization of 682
4. ???
5. Neutralization!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is a pun on the real world People's Liberation Army which is the national army of the People's Republic of China.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm thinking that, with a bit of work, this (The People's Liberation Army) could be a new GOI. I'm thinking that their goal would be to "free humanity from [stuff that limits freedom, such as dictatorships] & ignorance [such as the Foundation withholding information from the public (thereby being an enemy, but a benevolent one, of the Foundation)]".

Woops, just realized that it's the People's LibRARation (as in library) Army, not the People's Liberation (as in liberty) Army XD, although the above idea could still be good.

Wow, this post looks like it was just hit by the Elaboration Squirrel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconding this. Really liked that part

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"People's Librariation Army"

…..
….
…
..
.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This isn't a great skip, but I'll give it a hesitant upvote for the letter at the end. The paper really helped humanize the SCP, but that last annotation was very unnecessary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was extremely on the fence throughout, but The People's Librariation Army got me (though I would suggest Libraration as an improvement, as it's the same number of syllables as liberation, and I had the devil of a time trying to pronounce Librariation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: On this read-through, it doesn't do a lot for me. Still, I'm going to give eight-months-ago me the benefit of the doubt and just cancel my vote instead of downvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was my favourite part, too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. The last note seems a bit awkward but that's probably just me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a short story to 1230's novel. Different, but interesting in their own ways. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It seems like a less interesting version of 1230.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was able to affect them like that because a printer happened to be within its range before they knew about that effect. As long as it's kept in that area and away from other printers, it's Safe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was thinking at first that it just infected people with Fred, but that last note is really good and making me reconsider my opinion of the entire piece.

One question: why isn't this Euclid? It can affect things outside its containment, considering that it successfully brainwashed a group of agents and researches to provide it with more printers. Just because it says it won't do that again doesn't exactly mean it should be believed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoy this. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It feels like a cross of several ideas, but I love the idea of a seemingly inanimate SCP object demonstrating that it's fully aware of its containment and apologizing for giving us trouble. Plus the "People's Libraritarian Army" is a fun touch.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like I said earlier, I like this. Good job, Azzle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, I was a bit creeped out by "…like having brown hear or wearing glass." I have both, and I thought it was going to be that type of article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It seems a bit too much like a cross between SCP-423 "Fred" and SCP-1318 "Frankie" in that it appears in random texts and it makes you talk to yourself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, this started out feeling very much like Gollum/Smeagol talking to himself/themselves, but that last document is… intriguing. I would probably downvote if it was just the double personality thing, but I'm not sure now.
